walkthrough.title: Place the Main Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Barcelona set up. First, place the main board in the middle of the table. This is the large board showing the grid of streets and blocks that represent the Eixample, Barcelona's 19th-century expansion district. You will see spaces for buildings, street crossings, and the Citizen tracks along the edges.

walkthrough.title: Place Building Bonus Tiles
walkthrough.text: Find the four Building Bonus tiles. Place one in each of the four dedicated spaces on the main board. These tiles reward the first player to construct a building in each row with 5 Victory Points. They start face up and get flipped when claimed.

walkthrough.title: Place Action Tiles
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 11 Action tiles and place one on each Action tile space on the main board. These determine which actions are available at each street. For your first game, you can skip this step and use the actions printed directly on the board instead.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Citizen Tracks
walkthrough.text: Now set up the Citizen tracks on the main board. In a game with fewer than 4 players, place Citizen tokens of the corresponding type on the marked spaces. In a 3-player game, cover spaces marked with a 3. In a 2-player game, cover both the 3 and 2 spaces. This adjusts the game length and scoring pace for different player counts.

walkthrough.title: Fill the Cloth Bag with Citizens
walkthrough.text: Place all remaining Citizen tokens into the cloth bag. There are three types of Citizens: 25 Working Class, 24 Middle Class, and 23 Upper Class -- 72 tokens total. You will draw from this bag throughout the game. Give it a good shake to mix them up.

walkthrough.title: Place Cerda Scoring Tiles
walkthrough.text: Randomly select 3 of the 14 Cerda Scoring tiles and place them on their designated spaces along the Citizen tracks on the main board. These tiles determine what gets scored during the three Cerda Scoring phases. Return the unused tiles to the box.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Side Board
walkthrough.text: Place the side board next to the main board. This holds the Modernisme tile display and the Sidewalk for Cobblestone placement. Remove the 3 Modernisme tiles that correspond to the 3 Cerda Scoring tiles you placed -- return them to the box. Then shuffle the remaining Modernisme tiles (from the pool of 18) into a face-down stack on the side board. Reveal the top 4 tiles and place them on their reserved display spaces.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Public Service Tiles
walkthrough.text: Separate the 21 Public Service tiles by type -- there are 7 different types with 3 tiles each. Stack each type in descending order of Coin cost, with the 3-Coin tile on top. Randomly select 5 of these 7 stacks and place them on the side board. Return the other 2 types to the box. In a 2-player game, also remove the cheapest tile from each stack.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Building Tile Stacks
walkthrough.text: Separate all the Building tiles by type and create four stacks: 12 Level-1 Building tiles, 8 Level-2 Building tiles, 7 Level-3 Building tiles, and 8 Corner Building tiles. Place them with the non-building side (showing a cost on the left and benefits on the right) facing up. Set them near the board where everyone can reach.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Sagrada Familia Tiles
walkthrough.text: Separate the 16 Sagrada Familia tiles by level (indicated by the number printed on each tile) and place them near the board. Players will select these tiles as they advance on their personal Sagrada Familia track.

walkthrough.title: Create the General Supply
walkthrough.text: Create two separate piles of Coins and Cloth tokens next to the main board. These form the general supply. There are 30 Coins and 30 Cloth tokens. Both resources are intended to be unlimited -- if you run out of either, use any suitable substitute.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Player Board
walkthrough.text: Each player takes a player board and all components in their chosen color. Place your player board in front of you. Then place a small disk on the leftmost space of the Sagrada Familia track on your player board. Place 5 cubes next to the Modernisme Project spaces -- one on each bottom space to the right. These are your Modernisme markers.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Tram and Passengers
walkthrough.text: Place your Tram piece and your remaining 5 cubes -- called your Passengers -- on the corresponding spaces of the Tram area on your player board. Your Tram starts on your board and will be placed onto the main board the first time you take the Move Your Tram action.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Street Tiles
walkthrough.text: Each player gets 10 Narrow Street tiles and 5 Wide Street tiles. Create a stack of 4 Narrow Street tiles on the leftmost dedicated space below the Tram area. Place the remaining 6 Narrow Street tiles in a stack on the next space to the right. Then create a stack of all 5 Wide Street tiles on the next space. These stacks matter -- when a stack empties, you advance on the Cerda track.

walkthrough.title: Place Intersections and Warehouse
walkthrough.text: Place all 5 of your Intersection tiles on their dedicated spaces on your player board. Then set up your Warehouse: take 1 Coin and 1 Cloth token and place each on a separate leftmost Warehouse space. Finally, place a Cobblestone tile on each of the 6 rightmost spaces of your Warehouse. You start with 6 Cobblestones blocking storage space.

walkthrough.title: Place Your Building Markers
walkthrough.text: Take your 8 large disks -- these are your Building markers. Place them on the spaces at the bottom of your player board: create three stacks of 2 disks, then place 2 single disks on the last spots. When stacks empty during the game, you advance on the Cerda track.

walkthrough.title: Place Scoring and Track Markers
walkthrough.text: Place your last large disk (the Scoring marker) on the 0 space of the scoring track around the main board. Place your last small disk on the starting space of the Cerda track on the main board. Everyone should now have markers on both the scoring track and the Cerda track.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player and Draw Citizens
walkthrough.text: Determine the first player however you prefer and give them the First Player token. Finally, each player draws 2 Citizen tokens from the bag and places them face down near their player board. You may look at your own Citizens at any time, but keep them hidden from other players. You are ready to play.

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Barcelona is played over a variable number of rounds interrupted by three Cerda Scoring phases, followed by a Final Scoring. Every round, each player takes a single turn consisting of placing Citizens, performing actions, constructing a building, and drawing new Citizens. Play goes clockwise starting from the first player. At the end of the game, the player with the most Victory Points wins.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure: Four Mandatory Steps
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you must perform four steps in order. Step 1: Place 2 Citizens on a street crossing and optionally perform the crossing's actions. Step 2: Construct a Building using adjacent Citizens. Step 3: Check if a Cerda Scoring has been triggered. Step 4: Draw 2 new Citizens from the bag for your next turn. You must fully complete one action before starting another -- you cannot interrupt an action to gain resources for it.

walkthrough.title: Step 1: Place Citizens on a Crossing
walkthrough.text: Take your 2 Citizens and place them in an order of your choosing on a single street crossing that currently has no Citizens on it. If the crossing has a printed Coin cost, you must pay it first. After placing your Citizens, you may perform any or all of the actions corresponding to the streets meeting at that crossing. You can perform the vertical street action, the horizontal street action, and if the crossing intersects the diagonal street, that action too. Each action may be performed once, in any order.

walkthrough.title: Action: Gain Cloth or Coins
walkthrough.text: This action gives you a choice: either gain 1 Cloth and 3 Victory Points, or gain 2 Coins. Coins and Cloth must be stored in your Warehouse on your player board. Each Warehouse space holds exactly 1 Coin or 1 Cloth. Any resources you cannot store must be returned to the general supply immediately. You may return Coins or Cloth from your Warehouse to make space at any time.

walkthrough.title: Action: Place Cobblestone
walkthrough.text: Take the leftmost Cobblestone tile from your Warehouse and place it on the Sidewalk section of the side board onto an empty space that is orthogonally adjacent to at least one other Cobblestone. Immediately gain the benefits printed on the space you covered. Removing Cobblestones from your Warehouse frees up storage space for Coins and Cloth, and at game end you score Victory Points equal to the highest uncovered value on your Warehouse.

walkthrough.title: Action: Build Wide or Narrow Streets
walkthrough.text: Build 1 Wide Street tile or 2 Narrow Street tiles. Take them from your player board (from the leftmost stack if more than one) and place them on empty dedicated street spaces on the main board. After placing a Street tile, gain the printed benefits you covered. Then count the number of connected Street tiles in the same straight line as the one you just placed (ownership does not matter) and gain Victory Points: 2 VP per tile for Wide Streets, 1 VP per tile for Narrow Streets. When a stack of Street tiles on your player board empties, advance on the Cerda track: 1 step for a Narrow Street stack, 2 steps for the Wide Street stack.

walkthrough.title: Action: Build an Intersection
walkthrough.text: Take the leftmost Intersection from your player board, pay its depicted cost if applicable, and place it on a street crossing that does not already have an Intersection. If the space has an extra printed cost, pay that too. After placing, gain all printed adjacent benefits on the main board that are still visible. The real power of Intersections is ongoing: whenever any player places Citizens on one of your Intersections, you receive benefits. You can choose 1 benefit at first, 2 different benefits once you have built your 3rd Intersection, and up to 3 different benefits once you have built your 5th.

walkthrough.title: Action: Take a Modernisme Tile
walkthrough.text: Take one of the 4 available face-up Modernisme tiles from the side board and place it on an empty Modernisme Project space on your player board. Some spaces have an additional Cloth cost you must pay. You cannot take this action if you have no empty spaces. Immediately refill the display after taking a tile. If the stack runs out, reshuffle discarded tiles. Each Modernisme tile lists a scoring condition and VP multiplier that scores at end of game.

walkthrough.title: Action: Improve a Modernisme Project Space
walkthrough.text: Move exactly one Modernisme marker from its bottom position to its top position on your player board. Some spaces have an additional Cloth cost. A marker in its top position always lets a Modernisme tile score more times than in its bottom position. You can move a marker even if there is no Modernisme tile in that space yet.

walkthrough.title: Action: Build a Public Service
walkthrough.text: Spend 3, 2, or 1 Coins to build one of the available Public Service tiles. You can only build a Public Service type you have not yet built. Take the top tile of the chosen type, pay its cost, and place it next to your player board. Gain the printed Victory Points (10, 6, or 3 VP -- decreasing as more of that type are built by all players), advance 2 steps on the Cerda track, and gain the tile's main benefit, which is a powerful special action. Then flip the tile. There are 7 Public Service types, including Sant Antoni Market, Estacio de Franca, Hospital de La Santa Creu, and Universitat de Barcelona.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move Your Tram
walkthrough.text: The first time you take this action, place your Tram on any street space on the main board with no other Tram or Passenger. Every subsequent time, move it 1 or 2 street spaces away. Movements through your own Street tiles are free and do not count against the 2-space limit. You cannot end on a space with another Tram, backtrack, or stay in place. After moving, you may optionally place a Passenger by paying its cost from your player board. Then you may perform the action associated with that street. If you place a Passenger on another player's Street tile, that player scores as if they just placed that Street tile.

walkthrough.title: Step 2: Construct a Building
walkthrough.text: After finishing your actions, you must construct one Building if you are able to. A building requires specific Citizens to be present on the street crossings surrounding the block. Only visible (top of stack) Citizens count. To build: place a Building tile on an eligible space, claim the Building Bonus if it is the first in that row (5 VP), remove the required Citizens and place them on the Citizen tracks, score VP equal to the lowest visible number among all Citizen tracks, apply the building's benefits, flip the tile, and place a Building marker from your player board on it.

walkthrough.title: Building Types
walkthrough.text: There are four types of buildings. Level-1 Buildings require any 2 Citizens, have buildings on 2 sides with parks on the other 2, and advance you 1 step on the Cerda track. Level-2 Buildings require 1 Middle-Class Citizen and 1 other Citizen, have buildings on 3 sides, advance you 1 step on the Sagrada Familia track, but move you back 1 step on the Cerda track. Level-3 Buildings require 1 Upper-Class Citizen and any 2 other Citizens, have buildings on all 4 sides, advance you twice on the Sagrada Familia track with 7 bonus VP, but move you back 2 steps on the Cerda track. Corner Buildings require any 2 Citizens and can only be built on the triangular spaces next to the diagonal street.

walkthrough.title: Overbuilding
walkthrough.text: You may build Level-2 Buildings on top of already-built Level-1 Buildings, and Level-3 Buildings on top of already-built Level-1 or Level-2 Buildings. Follow the same construction process, but place the new tile on top of the old one. Stack Building markers in order. Important: Corner Buildings and Level-3 Buildings cannot be overbuilt.

walkthrough.title: Citizen Tracks and Cerda Scoring
walkthrough.text: As players construct Buildings, Citizens are placed on the Citizen tracks. Each type (Working, Middle, Upper Class) has its own track. When a section of a Citizen track fills to or beyond a marked Cerda Scoring space at the end of a player's turn, all players score that Cerda Scoring tile. Your position on the Cerda track determines your multiplier: between 0x and 4x. After the 1st and 2nd scoring, all players who advanced beyond the starting position reset their Cerda track markers. After the 3rd scoring, markers stay in place.

walkthrough.title: The Cerda Track
walkthrough.text: Various actions move you forward or backward on the Cerda track. Building Level-1 Buildings and emptying Street or Building marker stacks move you forward. Building Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings move you back. Public Services always advance you 2 steps. If you are at the bottom and must move back further, ignore the movement. If you are at the top and must advance further, gain 2 VP per forfeited step instead. Your position on this track determines your multiplier during Cerda Scorings.

walkthrough.title: The Sagrada Familia Track
walkthrough.text: When you advance on the Sagrada Familia track (primarily from Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings, and from the Museu del Modernisme Public Service), if you cross over a dedicated slot, select a Sagrada Familia tile of the corresponding level and place it in the slot. Immediately gain the benefits of that tile. The 16 tiles are separated by level and provide various resources and VP.

walkthrough.title: Step 4: Draw 2 New Citizens
walkthrough.text: At the end of your turn, draw 2 Citizen tokens at random from the bag. Place them face down near your player board. You can look at them at any time, but keep them hidden from other players. These are the Citizens you will place on your next turn.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends when the last (third) section of any Citizen track fills to its Cerda Scoring space at the end of a player's turn. All players score the 3rd Cerda Scoring tile, then play continues until everyone has played the same number of turns. Final Scoring adds three categories: VP for Cobblestones removed (rightmost visible number on Warehouse), VP for Passengers placed (rightmost uncovered Passenger space), and VP from each Modernisme tile multiplied by its corresponding marker position. The player with the most VP wins. Tiebreakers in order: furthest on the Cerda track, furthest on the Sagrada Familia track, most Building markers on the main board. If still tied, players share the victory.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Variable number of rounds, interrupted by 3 Cerda Scoring phases
Clockwise turns starting from first player
Most Victory Points at the end wins

summary.title: Turn Structure: Four Mandatory Steps
bullets: Step 1: Place 2 Citizens on a street crossing, perform actions
Step 2: Construct a Building (mandatory if possible)
Step 3: Check for Cerda Scoring trigger
Step 4: Draw 2 new Citizens from the bag

summary.title: Step 1: Place Citizens on a Crossing
bullets: Place 2 Citizens on a single empty crossing (pay printed cost if any)
May perform up to 3 actions: vertical, horizontal, and diagonal street
Each action once, in any order

summary.title: Action: Gain Cloth or Coins
bullets: Choice: 1 Cloth + 3 VP, or 2 Coins
Resources must fit in Warehouse (1 per space)
May discard from Warehouse to make room at any time

summary.title: Action: Place Cobblestone
bullets: Take leftmost Cobblestone from Warehouse, place on Sidewalk
Must be orthogonally adjacent to an existing Cobblestone
Gain printed benefit; frees Warehouse space; scores end-game VP

summary.title: Action: Build Wide or Narrow Streets
bullets: Build 1 Wide Street tile OR 2 Narrow Street tiles from player board
Gain covered benefits + VP per connected tile (2 VP Wide, 1 VP Narrow)
Emptying a stack: +1 Cerda step (Narrow) or +2 steps (Wide)

summary.title: Action: Build an Intersection
bullets: Pay cost, place on empty crossing, gain adjacent visible benefits
Ongoing: when any player places Citizens on your Intersection, gain benefits
1 Intersection = 1 benefit; 3 built = 2 benefits; 5 built = 3 benefits

summary.title: Action: Take a Modernisme Tile
bullets: Take 1 of 4 face-up tiles, place on empty Modernisme Project space
Pay Cloth cost if depicted; refill display immediately
Tile lists end-game scoring condition and VP multiplier

summary.title: Action: Improve a Modernisme Project Space
bullets: Move 1 Modernisme marker from bottom to top position
Pay Cloth cost if depicted
Top position = higher scoring multiplier at end of game

summary.title: Action: Build a Public Service
bullets: Pay 3/2/1 Coins for top tile of a type you have not built
Gain 10/6/3 VP (decreasing per tile built of that type)
Advance 2 steps on Cerda track + gain powerful special action
7 types including Sant Antoni Market, Estacio de Franca, Hospital, Universitat

summary.title: Action: Move Your Tram
bullets: First time: place Tram on any empty street space
Later: move 1-2 spaces (own Street tiles are free movement)
Optionally place a Passenger (pay cost) for an extra action
Passenger on another player's Street = that player rescores it

summary.title: Step 2: Construct a Building
bullets: Mandatory if possible -- place tile, remove adjacent Citizens to tracks
Score VP = lowest visible number among all Citizen tracks
Claim Building Bonus (5 VP) if first building in a row
Place Building marker from player board onto the tile

summary.title: Building Types
bullets: Level-1: any 2 Citizens, 2 built sides, +1 Cerda step
Level-2: 1 Middle-Class + 1 any, 3 built sides, +1 Sagrada / -1 Cerda
Level-3: 1 Upper-Class + 2 any, 4 built sides, +2 Sagrada +7 VP / -2 Cerda
Corner: any 2 Citizens, triangle spaces on diagonal only, cannot overbuild

summary.title: Overbuilding
bullets: Level-2 can overbuild Level-1; Level-3 can overbuild Level-1 or Level-2
Stack new tile and marker on top of old ones
Corner Buildings and Level-3 Buildings cannot be overbuilt

summary.title: Citizen Tracks and Cerda Scoring
bullets: Citizens placed on tracks when used to build
Track section fills to Cerda space = all players score that Cerda tile
Cerda track position determines multiplier (0x to 4x)
After 1st and 2nd scoring: reset Cerda markers; after 3rd: markers stay

summary.title: The Cerda Track
bullets: Advance: Level-1 build, empty stacks, Public Services (+2)
Move back: Level-2 (-1), Level-3 (-2)
At bottom: ignore further backward movement
At top: gain 2 VP per forfeited forward step

summary.title: The Sagrada Familia Track
bullets: Advance from Level-2 (+1), Level-3 (+2), and Museu Public Service
Cross a slot = select Sagrada Familia tile of matching level
Gain tile benefits immediately (resources, VP)

summary.title: Step 4: Draw 2 New Citizens
bullets: Draw 2 random Citizens from the bag
Keep face down near your board; look at them anytime
Hidden from other players

summary.title: Game End and Final Scoring
bullets: Triggers when 3rd Citizen track section fills; finish equal turns
Final scoring: Cobblestones VP + Passengers VP + Modernisme tile VP (condition x marker)
Tiebreak: Cerda track > Sagrada Familia track > Building markers on board

walkthrough.title: Welcome and Meet the Players
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with 3 example players -- Blue, Orange, and Purple -- the recommended player count for Barcelona. Each player will take different actions so you can see how everything connects. Follow along even if you have a different number of players at your table.

walkthrough.title: Starting Resources
walkthrough.text: Each player starts with 2 Citizens drawn from the bag, 1 Coin and 1 Cloth in their Warehouse, 6 Cobblestones blocking Warehouse spaces, and all their Street tiles, Intersection tiles, Building markers, Passengers, and Tram on their player board. The Cerda track markers all begin at the starting position. Everyone's Modernisme markers start on the bottom positions of their 5 project spaces.

walkthrough.title: Blue's Turn: Place Citizens
walkthrough.text: Blue goes first. Blue drew a Working-Class Citizen and a Middle-Class Citizen. Blue places both on a street crossing near the center of the board -- a good strategic position with access to useful actions. This crossing has no printed cost, so placement is free. Blue stacks the Middle-Class Citizen on top, since that one might be needed for a Level-2 Building later.

walkthrough.title: Blue's Actions: Gain Coins and Build Streets
walkthrough.text: This crossing gives Blue access to two actions. First, Blue takes the Gain Cloth/Coins action and chooses 2 Coins, filling two Warehouse spaces. Then Blue takes the Build Narrow Streets action, placing 2 Narrow Street tiles from the leftmost stack on the player board onto the main board. Blue covers a benefit space and gains 1 Coin, plus VP for the connected street length. Smart play -- Blue is stockpiling resources while building a street presence.

walkthrough.title: Blue Constructs a Building
walkthrough.text: Now Blue must construct a Building. Blue spots a block adjacent to the crossing where Citizens were just placed. The 2 Citizens there satisfy the requirement for a Level-1 Building (any 2 Citizens). Blue places a Level-1 Building tile, moves the 2 Citizens to their respective Citizen tracks, scores VP equal to the lowest visible Citizen track number, advances 1 step on the Cerda track, flips the tile, and places a Building marker. This is also the first building in its row, so Blue claims the Building Bonus tile for 5 VP.

walkthrough.title: Why That Opening Was Effective
walkthrough.text: Blue accomplished a lot in one turn: gained resources, placed Street tiles for VP and future Tram movement, built a Level-1 Building for VP and a Cerda track advance, and claimed a Building Bonus. The key insight is that everything in Barcelona feeds into everything else. Streets make your Tram faster. Buildings move Citizens to tracks which trigger scorings. The Cerda track multiplies your scoring tile rewards. Blue is setting up multiple engines at once.

walkthrough.title: Orange's Turn: Place Citizens and Take Actions
walkthrough.text: Orange drew 2 Working-Class Citizens. Orange places them on a crossing with access to a Place Cobblestone action and a Gain Cloth/Coins action. Orange first takes the Gain Cloth/Coins action, choosing 1 Cloth and 3 VP. Then Orange takes the Place Cobblestone action, removing the leftmost Cobblestone from the Warehouse and placing it on the Sidewalk, gaining the printed benefit. This frees up a Warehouse space for future storage.

walkthrough.title: Orange Constructs a Level-1 Building
walkthrough.text: Orange builds a Level-1 Building using the 2 Working-Class Citizens just placed. Orange moves them to the Working-Class track, scores VP from the Citizen tracks, advances 1 step on the Cerda track, and places a Building marker. Solid turn -- Orange cleared Cobblestones for Warehouse space while building.

walkthrough.title: Purple's Turn: The Mistake -- Ignoring Resources
walkthrough.text: Purple drew a Middle-Class Citizen and an Upper-Class Citizen -- great Citizens for higher-level buildings. Excited by the Upper-Class Citizen, Purple places both on a crossing with access to an Improve Modernisme Project Space action and a Take Modernisme Tile action. Purple takes the Modernisme tile, paying 1 Cloth from the Warehouse. Then Purple tries to Improve a Modernisme Project space but the space costs 2 Cloth, and Purple only has 0 Cloth left. Purple cannot afford it and wastes the action opportunity.

walkthrough.title: Purple's Building Problem
walkthrough.text: Now Purple must build. Purple has a Middle-Class Citizen and an Upper-Class Citizen on the crossing, which would satisfy a Level-2 Building (1 Middle-Class + 1 any). But Purple placed Citizens on a crossing surrounded by blocks that already have buildings, or the blocks are not adjacent to enough Citizens. Purple ends up building a basic Level-1 Building in a less ideal spot. Purple misses the Building Bonus because Blue already claimed it for that row.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Plan Your Crossing Before Placing Citizens
walkthrough.text: Purple's mistake was twofold. First, Purple spent resources on Modernisme tiles without checking if there was enough Cloth to also Improve the project space -- wasting half the turn's action potential. Second, Purple placed Citizens on a crossing without considering what building could be constructed nearby. In Barcelona, you must think backwards: decide what building you want to construct, find a block with the right Citizens available, and only then choose your crossing. Actions are a bonus; building is mandatory.

walkthrough.title: Blue's Second Turn: Tram and Intersection
walkthrough.text: Blue draws 2 new Citizens and places them on a different crossing. This time Blue takes the Build an Intersection action, paying the cost and placing an Intersection tile on a busy crossing. Blue gains adjacent benefits -- 2 Coins and a Cerda track advance. Then Blue takes the Move Your Tram action for the first time, placing the Tram on a street space with one of Blue's own Street tiles. Blue decides not to place a Passenger yet to save resources.

walkthrough.title: Why Intersections Are Powerful
walkthrough.text: Blue's Intersection is now on the board. From this point on, whenever any player -- Blue, Orange, or Purple -- places Citizens on that crossing, Blue gets to choose a benefit from their built Intersection abilities. This is passive income that triggers on other players' turns. The more Intersections you build, the more benefits you receive per trigger: 1 benefit with 1-2 Intersections, 2 benefits with 3-4, and 3 benefits with all 5. Intersections are one of the strongest engines in Barcelona.

walkthrough.title: Orange's Second Turn: Building Streets Strategically
walkthrough.text: Orange places Citizens and takes the Build Wide Street action, placing 1 Wide Street tile. Orange chooses a spot that extends an existing line of street tiles, earning 2 VP per connected tile -- that is 4 VP for a line of 2 tiles. Orange also takes a Gain Coins action for 2 more Coins, then builds a Level-1 Building. Orange is setting up a long street for future scoring.

walkthrough.title: Purple's Mistake: Forgetting the Cerda Track
walkthrough.text: Purple places Citizens and takes two resource-gathering actions. Purple then builds a Level-2 Building, which requires 1 Middle-Class Citizen and 1 other Citizen. Purple gains 1 step on the Sagrada Familia track and a Sagrada Familia tile with nice benefits. However, Purple moves back 1 step on the Cerda track as the Level-2 penalty. Purple is now behind the starting position on the Cerda track. When the first Cerda Scoring triggers, Purple's multiplier will be 0x -- scoring zero points from the scoring tile.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Balance the Cerda Track
walkthrough.text: The Cerda track is critical because it multiplies your Cerda Scoring tile rewards by 0x to 4x. Building Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings moves you backward. If you build higher-level buildings without also advancing (through Level-1 builds, emptying stacks, or Public Services), your multiplier drops to 0 and you score nothing from the scoring phases. Always check your Cerda track position before committing to a higher-level build. One Level-1 Building per Level-2 Building is a good rule of thumb to stay balanced.

walkthrough.title: Purple's Mistake: Hoarding Citizens
walkthrough.text: Over the next two turns, Purple draws great Citizens -- another Upper-Class and two Middle-Class -- but keeps placing them on crossings where the building options are poor. Purple has been choosing crossings for their action icons rather than for their building potential. Purple has now placed Citizens on 3 crossings but only constructed 2 buildings. Meanwhile, Blue and Orange have each built 3 buildings and are filling the Citizen tracks faster, pushing toward a Cerda Scoring.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Building is Not Optional
walkthrough.text: In Barcelona, constructing a Building is mandatory whenever you can. If Citizens are available on crossings surrounding a buildable block, you must build. This means you should always plan your Citizen placement around where you want to build, not the other way around. Actions are nice, but they are secondary. The building step drives the game -- it moves Citizens to tracks, triggers scoring, and generates most of your VP. Never sacrifice a good building opportunity for a slightly better action.

walkthrough.title: First Cerda Scoring Triggers
walkthrough.text: At the end of Blue's turn, the Working-Class Citizen track fills to its first Cerda Scoring space. All players now score the first Cerda Scoring tile. Blue has been advancing on the Cerda track and sits at the x3 multiplier -- scoring the tile 3 times. Orange is at x2 -- scoring it twice. Purple, stuck behind the starting position from Level-2 building penalties, is at x0 -- scoring nothing. The gap is significant. After scoring, Blue's and Orange's markers reset to the starting position.

walkthrough.title: After the First Scoring: Modernisme Refresh
walkthrough.text: After the Cerda Scoring, all Modernisme tiles on display are discarded and 4 new tiles are revealed. This means the available scoring options change after each scoring phase. Players who have been waiting to pick Modernisme tiles now have fresh options. The game continues with the next player's turn.

walkthrough.title: Continue Playing
walkthrough.text: Play continues clockwise. Each turn follows the same four steps: place 2 Citizens, perform actions, construct a Building, draw 2 new Citizens. As the game progresses, Citizens fill the tracks toward the 2nd and 3rd Cerda Scoring spaces. Build Streets to extend lines for growing VP rewards. Place Intersections for passive income. Collect Modernisme tiles and Improve their markers for end-game scoring. Build Public Services for big VP and Cerda track advances. The game ends when the 3rd Citizen track section fills.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen every major action in Barcelona, watched Purple learn from three common mistakes, and experienced a Cerda Scoring phase. Remember: plan your crossing placement around building opportunities, keep your Cerda track position healthy before building Level-2 or Level-3 Buildings, and manage your Warehouse resources so you can afford actions when they matter. You are ready to play a full game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Build
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First -- check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second -- reference the Appendix for a quick guide to actions, building types, and game terms.

And most importantly -- ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Build
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for actions, building types, and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide -- tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the most common mistakes new players make. First, choosing crossings for actions and forgetting that building is mandatory -- you end up with Citizens stranded on crossings with no good building options. Second, building Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings without advancing the Cerda track to compensate, leading to a 0x multiplier during Cerda Scorings. Third, filling your Warehouse with Cobblestones and having no room for Coins and Cloth when you need them. Fourth, ignoring the Modernisme tile display and missing high-value end-game scoring opportunities. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the biggest beginner traps.

walkthrough.title: Think Backwards: Building First, Actions Second
walkthrough.text: The single most important habit in Barcelona is to plan each turn starting from the building step. Before you place your Citizens, ask yourself: what building do I want to construct, and where are the Citizens I need? Find a block with the right mix of Citizens on surrounding crossings, choose a crossing that gives you access to useful actions, and only then place your Citizens. If you pick a crossing purely for its actions, you might end up unable to build or forced into a bad building. Actions are the bonus. Building is the core.

walkthrough.title: Cerda Track Management
walkthrough.text: The Cerda track is deceptively important. It multiplies your Cerda Scoring tile reward between 0x and 4x. Building Level-1 Buildings and emptying Street or Building marker stacks move you forward. Building Level-2 Buildings costs 1 step back and Level-3 costs 2 steps back. Public Services always give you 2 steps forward. The key is balance: do not build Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings back-to-back without advancing first. Aim to be at x2 or higher before each Cerda Scoring. Reaching x4 can mean scoring a tile four times, which is often worth 20 or more VP.

walkthrough.title: Warehouse Management
walkthrough.text: Your Warehouse starts with only 2 free spaces (1 Coin and 1 Cloth) because 6 Cobblestones block the rest. You need Coins to build Public Services, pay Intersection costs, and access certain crossings. You need Cloth for Modernisme tiles and Improving project spaces. Placing Cobblestones on the Sidewalk frees spaces but costs actions. The right pace is to remove Cobblestones steadily -- about 1-2 per round -- so you always have room to store resources when you gain them. Do not wait until late game to start clearing Cobblestones.

walkthrough.title: Intersection Strategy
walkthrough.text: Intersections are the game's best passive income engine. Once placed, they trigger every time any player places Citizens on that crossing -- including your opponents. Place Intersections on high-traffic crossings where other players are likely to build. Building your 3rd Intersection unlocks a 2nd benefit choice per trigger, and your 5th unlocks a 3rd. Aim to build 3 Intersections in the early-to-mid game. The benefits compound: a well-placed Intersection on a popular crossing can trigger 5 or more times over the course of a game.

walkthrough.title: Street Building for VP
walkthrough.text: Streets score VP based on connected tiles in a straight line: 1 VP per tile for Narrow Streets and 2 VP per tile for Wide Streets. This means long connected lines score exponentially more than scattered placements. A line of 4 Wide Street tiles scores 8 VP each time a new tile extends it, plus the player who triggers it via a Passenger also causes it to rescore. Plan your streets in lines, not scattered across the board. Also remember that emptying a Street tile stack advances you on the Cerda track -- time your stack depletion before a Cerda Scoring if you can.

walkthrough.title: Modernisme Scoring Explained
walkthrough.text: Modernisme tiles are a major end-game scoring category. Each tile lists a condition (like VP per Wide Street tile you placed, or VP per Public Service built) and you multiply it by your Modernisme marker position. The marker starts in the bottom position and can be improved to the top position with an action. A tile scoring 2 VP per Wide Street tile with a marker at x4 position and 3 Wide Streets placed would score 2 x 3 x 4 = 24 VP. Pick tiles that align with what you are already doing, and make sure to Improve the marker on your highest-scoring tile.

walkthrough.title: Diversify Your Scoring
walkthrough.text: The highest-scoring players in Barcelona collect VP from every source. In-game VP comes from actions, building, street connections, and Cerda Scorings. End-game VP comes from Cobblestones, Passengers, and Modernisme tiles. A player who scores heavily from buildings but ignores Modernisme will lose to a player who has a moderate building score plus 20 to 30 VP from well-chosen Modernisme tiles. Make sure you are not leaving entire scoring categories on the table. Even investing a couple of actions in Cobblestone placement can yield 7 VP and free up crucial Warehouse space.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Choosing crossings for actions without planning the building step
Building Level-2/3 without advancing Cerda track = 0x multiplier
Ignoring Warehouse space -- Cobblestones block Coin and Cloth storage
Missing Modernisme tiles = missing major end-game scoring

bullets: Plan each turn from the building step backward
Find the block you want to build, identify needed Citizens
Choose a crossing that serves both building and actions

summary.title: Cerda Track Management

Level-1 builds, empty stacks, Public Services advance you
Level-2 (-1 step) and Level-3 (-2 steps) push you back
Aim for x2+ before each scoring; x4 can yield 20+ VP

summary.title: Warehouse Management
bullets: Start with only 2 free spaces; 6 Cobblestones block the rest
Remove Cobblestones steadily (1-2 per round) to free storage
Coins fund Public Services and Intersection costs
Cloth funds Modernisme tiles and Improve actions

summary.title: Intersection Strategy
bullets: Passive income: triggers when ANY player places Citizens on your Intersection
Place on high-traffic crossings for maximum triggers
3rd Intersection = 2 benefits per trigger; 5th = 3 benefits
Well-placed Intersection can trigger 5+ times per game

summary.title: Street Building for VP
bullets: VP = connected tiles in a line (1 VP/Narrow, 2 VP/Wide)
Long connected lines score much more than scattered placements
Emptying a Street stack advances Cerda track -- time it before scoring
Passengers on streets rescore them for the street owner

summary.title: Modernisme Scoring Explained
bullets: Condition x marker multiplier = end-game VP per tile
Pick tiles that align with your existing strategy
Improve the marker on your highest-value tile first
Example: 2 VP x 3 Wide Streets x 4 marker = 24 VP

summary.title: Diversify Your Scoring
bullets: In-game VP: actions, buildings, streets, Cerda Scorings
End-game VP: Cobblestones, Passengers, Modernisme tiles
Moderate building score + strong Modernisme can beat pure building
A few Cobblestone actions = 7 VP + freed Warehouse space

walkthrough.title: Intersection Engine Strategy
walkthrough.text: The Intersection-heavy approach is one of the strongest openings in Barcelona. The idea is to rush Intersections onto high-traffic crossings so you earn passive benefits every time any player places Citizens there. Focus your first 3-4 turns on gaining Coins (to pay Intersection costs) and building Intersections. Once you hit 3 Intersections, you receive 2 benefits per trigger. At 5, you get 3 benefits -- generating free Coins, Cloth, Cerda advances, and VP without spending actions. Pair this with Modernisme tiles that reward Intersection count for massive end-game scoring.

walkthrough.title: Positioning Intersections for Maximum Triggers
walkthrough.text: Not all crossings are equal. Place Intersections on crossings adjacent to empty blocks that multiple players will want to build on. Crossings near the center of the board see more traffic than edges. Crossings near the diagonal street are especially valuable because they offer 3 actions instead of 2. Watch where opponents are building and place your Intersections on crossings they are likely to use. Each trigger from an opponent is a free benefit you did not have to spend an action to earn.

walkthrough.title: Street Line Strategy
walkthrough.text: The street-focused approach builds long connected lines of Street tiles for escalating VP. Wide Streets score 2 VP per connected tile, so a line of 4 Wide Streets scores 8 VP each time it is extended. Narrow Streets score 1 VP per tile but you place 2 at a time, letting you build lines faster. The key is committing to one or two streets early and extending them consistently. Combine this with the Move Your Tram action to place Passengers on your own Street tiles, rescoring them and earning Passenger end-game VP. The Passeig de Lluis Companys Public Service lets you build streets with a bonus Cerda advance.

walkthrough.title: Tram and Passenger Strategy
walkthrough.text: The Tram is Barcelona's most flexible action because it lets you place Passengers that grant extra actions while scoring end-game VP. Moving through your own Street tiles is free, so a long line of your own streets lets your Tram cover huge distances. Place Passengers strategically: on your own Street tiles to rescore them, on crossings with valuable actions you want to perform again, or on opponents' Streets to gain the action while giving them VP. Time your Passenger placements to sync with actions you need -- a well-timed Passenger on a Gain Coins street before a Public Service purchase can be game-changing.

walkthrough.title: Sagrada Familia Track Rush
walkthrough.text: The Sagrada Familia track provides powerful tile bonuses as you advance. Each time you cross a tile slot, you select a Sagrada Familia tile and gain its benefits immediately -- resources, VP, or special abilities. Level-2 Buildings advance you 1 step and Level-3 Buildings advance you 2 steps. The Museu del Modernisme Catala Public Service also advances you 2 steps. A Sagrada Familia rush strategy builds Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings aggressively while using Public Services and Level-1 Buildings to compensate on the Cerda track. The tiles you collect can provide the resources needed to sustain the strategy.

walkthrough.title: Cerda Track Maximization
walkthrough.text: Advanced players treat the Cerda track as a scoring multiplier to be optimized. The track resets after the 1st and 2nd Cerda Scorings but stays in place after the 3rd. This means you should time your Cerda track advances to peak just before each scoring. Build Level-1 Buildings and empty stacks to advance, then build your Level-2 and Level-3 Buildings after a scoring when the track resets. If you are at the top of the track and need to advance further, you gain 2 VP per forfeited step -- making aggressive Cerda advancement valuable even when already maxed.

walkthrough.title: Public Service Timing
walkthrough.text: Public Services are among the most powerful single actions in the game: VP (10/6/3), a 2-step Cerda advance, and a strong special ability. The first tile of each type awards 10 VP and costs 3 Coins. Buying early maximizes VP but requires significant Coin investment. The third tile costs only 1 Coin but gives only 3 VP. The sweet spot is often the 2nd tile (2 Coins for 6 VP plus the Cerda advance and ability). The Estacio de Franca (free Tram + Passenger) and Hospital de La Santa Creu (free Intersection) Public Services are particularly strong because they combine the Public Service benefits with the Intersection or Tram engine.

walkthrough.title: Modernisme Tile Synergies
walkthrough.text: Modernisme tiles score based on a condition multiplied by your marker position. The strongest Modernisme plays come from stacking multiple tiles that reward the same strategy. If you are building Streets, take tiles that reward Narrow Street lines, Wide Street counts, and total Street tiles. If you focus on Intersections, take tiles that reward Intersections adjacent to 3+ Buildings and total Intersections placed. Always Improve the marker on your highest-value tile. A tile scoring 3 VP per condition at x4 marker is worth 12 VP per qualifying item. Two such tiles can easily generate 30-50 VP in end-game scoring.

walkthrough.title: Overbuilding for Maximum Value
walkthrough.text: Overbuilding -- placing a Level-2 on a Level-1 or a Level-3 on a Level-1/2 -- is a powerful but risky move. It scores VP again for the same block, advances the Sagrada Familia track, and lets you use Citizens that might otherwise be stranded. But it also costs Cerda track steps backward. The best overbuilding targets are blocks where you already have a Building marker, because you keep the marker stack and the block still counts for Modernisme tile conditions. Remember that Level-3 Buildings give 7 VP and two Sagrada Familia advances, making them strong late-game plays when the Cerda track penalty matters less.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Cerda Scoring Tiles
walkthrough.text: At setup, 3 of 14 Cerda Scoring tiles are placed on the board. Before making any strategic decisions, read all three and plan your game around them. If the tiles reward Corner Buildings, Street tiles, or Intersections, adjust your strategy accordingly. Since the tiles also remove the matching Modernisme tiles from the pool, the remaining Modernisme tiles are more likely to reward different conditions. The 3rd scoring tile is especially important because Cerda track positions do not reset afterward -- your final track position determines your multiplier for any remaining scoring and the tiebreaker.

Passeig de Lluis Companys Public Service adds Cerda advance

Matching Modernisme tiles are removed, altering the tile pool

Final track position also serves as first tiebreaker

entries.definition: The first step of every turn. Take your 2 Citizens and place them on a single street crossing with no Citizens on it. If the crossing has a printed Coin cost, pay it first. You then may perform the actions corresponding to the vertical, horizontal, and (if applicable) diagonal streets at that crossing.

The mandatory second step of every turn. If you are able to build, you must build. Select a block adjacent to Citizens that meet the building's requirements, remove those Citizens to the Citizen tracks, score VP equal to the lowest visible Citizen track value, apply building benefits, flip the tile, and place a Building marker.

The third step of every turn. Check if any Citizen track section has been filled to or beyond its marked Cerda Scoring space. If so, all players score that tile based on their Cerda track multiplier (0x to 4x). After the 1st and 2nd scorings, reset Cerda markers. After the 3rd, markers stay.
The fourth and final step of every turn. Draw 2 Citizen tokens at random from the cloth bag. Keep them face down near your player board, hidden from other players.

Action: choose either 1 Cloth and 3 Victory Points, or 2 Coins. Resources must be stored in your Warehouse. Each Warehouse space holds exactly 1 Coin or 1 Cloth. Excess resources are returned to the supply.

Action: take the leftmost Cobblestone tile from your Warehouse and place it on the Sidewalk section of the side board, orthogonally adjacent to at least one other Cobblestone. Gain the printed benefit of the covered space. Frees up Warehouse storage and scores end-game VP.

Action: build 1 Wide Street tile from your player board and place it on an empty dedicated Wide Street space on the main board. Gain the covered benefit, then score 2 VP per connected tile in the same straight line (any player's tiles count).

Action: build 2 Narrow Street tiles from your player board and place them on empty dedicated Narrow Street spaces on the main board. Gain covered benefits, then score 1 VP per connected tile per placement. The 2 tiles do not need to be adjacent.

Action: take the leftmost Intersection from your player board, pay its cost, and place it on a crossing without an existing Intersection. Pay any additional printed cost on the space. Gain all visible adjacent printed benefits. Ongoing: when any player places Citizens on your Intersection, you receive benefits (1/2/3 based on Intersections built: 1-2/3-4/5).

Action: take 1 of 4 face-up Modernisme tiles from the side board and place it on an empty Modernisme Project space on your player board. Pay Cloth cost if depicted. Cannot take if no empty space available. Refill display immediately.

Action: move exactly 1 Modernisme marker from its bottom position to its top position on your player board. Pay Cloth cost if depicted. A marker in its top position increases the scoring multiplier for that Modernisme tile at end of game.

Action: pay 3/2/1 Coins to build the top tile of a Public Service type you have not yet built. Gain 10/6/3 VP (decreasing as more of that type are built), advance 2 steps on the Cerda track, and gain the tile's powerful special action. There are 7 Public Service types.

Action: first time, place your Tram on any street space with no Tram or Passenger. Later, move 1-2 spaces (movement through your own Street tiles is free). After moving, optionally place a Passenger (pay its cost) to gain an extra action. Cannot end on another Tram, backtrack, or stay in place.
Requires any 2 Citizens. Has buildings on 2 sides of the block and parks on the other 2 sides. After building, advance 1 step on the Cerda track. Can be overbuilt by Level-2 or Level-3 Buildings.

Requires 1 Middle-Class Citizen and 1 other Citizen. Has buildings on 3 sides and a park on the 4th. After building, advance 1 step on the Sagrada Familia track (gaining benefits if applicable) and move 1 step back on the Cerda track. Can be overbuilt by Level-3 Buildings.

Requires 1 Upper-Class Citizen and any 2 other Citizens. Has buildings on all 4 sides. After building, advance twice on the Sagrada Familia track, gain 7 VP, and move 2 steps back on the Cerda track. Cannot be overbuilt.
Requires any 2 Citizens. Can only be built on the triangular spaces next to the diagonal street. Only the 3 surrounding crossings provide Citizens. Cannot be overbuilt.

Building a higher-level Building on top of an already-built lower-level Building. Level-2 can overbuild Level-1. Level-3 can overbuild Level-1 or Level-2. Place new tile on top, stack Building markers in order. Corner Buildings and Level-3 Buildings cannot be overbuilt.

One of the 3 Citizen types. There are 25 in the game. Can be used to fulfill any building requirement that calls for any Citizen. Placed on the Working-Class section of the Citizen track when used for construction.
One of the 3 Citizen types. There are 24 in the game. Required for Level-2 Buildings (1 Middle-Class + 1 any). Placed on the Middle-Class section of the Citizen track when used for construction.
One of the 3 Citizen types. There are 23 in the game. Required for Level-3 Buildings (1 Upper-Class + 2 any). Placed on the Upper-Class section of the Citizen track when used for construction.

Three tracks on the main board (Working, Middle, Upper Class) where Citizens are placed after being used to construct Buildings. Each track has sections separated by Cerda Scoring spaces. Filling a section triggers a Cerda Scoring.

A track on the main board showing multiplier positions from 0x to 4x. Various actions move you forward or backward. Your position determines your Cerda Scoring tile multiplier. Resets after 1st and 2nd scorings. If at top and advancing, gain 2 VP per forfeited step.

A personal track on your player board. Advance from Level-2 Buildings (+1), Level-3 Buildings (+2), and the Museu del Modernisme Public Service (+2). Crossing a tile slot lets you select and gain a Sagrada Familia tile of the corresponding level.
The track around the main board where each player's Scoring marker (large disk) records their VP throughout the game. Use +100/200/300/400 VP tiles if you pass the start.

A row of spaces on your player board for storing Coins and Cloth. Each space holds exactly 1 resource. Starts with 6 Cobblestones blocking spaces and 1 Coin + 1 Cloth pre-placed. Remove Cobblestones via the Place Cobblestone action to free storage.

Five spaces on your player board where Modernisme tiles are placed. Each has a Modernisme marker (cube) that starts in the bottom position. Some spaces have Cloth costs for placing tiles or improving markers. Tiles score end-game VP multiplied by the marker position.

A cube on your player board next to each Modernisme Project space. Starts in the bottom position. The Improve a Modernisme Project Space action moves it to the top position, increasing the end-game scoring multiplier for the corresponding Modernisme tile.

Large disks placed on Buildings you construct. You start with 8 (in stacks of 2, 2, 2, 1, 1). Emptying a stack advances you on the Cerda track. If you run out, you still build but do not place a marker. Building markers on the board determine presence in blocks for scoring.

A cube placed from your player board onto a street space containing your Tram. Pay the printed cost (Coins and/or Cloth) to place. Grants an extra action matching the street. If placed on another player's Street tile, that tile rescores for the owner. End-game VP based on rightmost uncovered Passenger space.

Your personal Tram piece. First placed on any street space; later moved 1-2 spaces (own Street tiles are free movement). Cannot end on another Tram, backtrack, or stay in place. After moving, you may optionally place a Passenger on the same space.

An intersection of streets on the main board where Citizens are placed. Each crossing connects to 2 or 3 streets (vertical, horizontal, and sometimes diagonal), each with its own action. Some crossings have printed Coin costs. An Intersection tile may be placed on a crossing.

The smallest square formed by two pairs of parallel intersecting streets. Each block can hold 1 non-Corner Building (which can be overbuilt). Blocks on the diagonal street can each hold 2 Corner Buildings. A block is where Building tiles are placed.
A special diagonal street crossing the grid. Crossings on the diagonal street offer 3 actions instead of 2. Corner Buildings can only be built on the triangular spaces next to the diagonal street.

A resource token stored in the Warehouse. Used to pay for Intersections, Public Services, certain crossing costs, and some Modernisme actions. Gained via the Gain Cloth/Coins action (2 Coins option) and various benefits.

A resource token stored in the Warehouse. Used to pay for Modernisme tiles, Improving Modernisme markers, and some Passenger costs. Gained via the Gain Cloth/Coins action (1 Cloth + 3 VP option) and various benefits.

Tiles that start on your Warehouse, blocking storage spaces. Removed via the Place Cobblestone action and placed on the Sidewalk section of the side board. Each removal frees a Warehouse space. End-game VP based on the rightmost visible VP value in your Warehouse.
A section on the side board where Cobblestone tiles are placed. Tiles must be placed orthogonally adjacent to at least one existing Cobblestone. Each space has a printed benefit gained when covered.

One of 10 per player (in stacks of 4 and 6 on the player board). Placed on dedicated Narrow Street spaces on the main board via the Build Narrow Streets action (2 at a time). Scores 1 VP per connected tile in a straight line. Emptying a stack advances Cerda track by 1 step.

One of 5 per player (1 stack on the player board). Placed on dedicated Wide Street spaces on the main board via the Build Wide Streets action (1 at a time). Scores 2 VP per connected tile in a straight line. Emptying the stack advances Cerda track by 2 steps.

One of 5 per player on the player board. Placed on street crossings via the Build an Intersection action. Gains adjacent visible benefits when placed. Ongoing: whenever any player places Citizens on the crossing, the Intersection owner receives benefits (1/2/3 based on total Intersections built).
One of 11 tiles placed on Action tile spaces on the main board during setup. They determine which actions correspond to which streets. For a first game, use the printed actions on the board instead.
Four tiles placed on the main board at setup. The first player to construct a building in each row gains 5 VP and flips the corresponding tile. Once claimed, the bonus is no longer available.

3 of 14 tiles randomly placed on the Citizen tracks during setup. Each shows a scoring condition and VP value. When a Citizen track section fills to its scoring space, all players score the tile based on their Cerda track multiplier. Flipped face down after scoring.

One of 18 tiles (minus 3 matching Cerda Scoring tiles). Taken from the 4-tile display on the side board and placed on Modernisme Project spaces on your player board. Each lists a scoring condition and VP value, scored at end of game multiplied by the corresponding marker position.

One of 16 tiles separated by level. Selected when advancing past a tile slot on the Sagrada Familia track. Placed in the slot just passed. Benefits (resources, VP) are gained immediately. Tile is flipped after resolution.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to gain 1 Cloth for every block with at least one of your Building markers, advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to perform a Move Your Tram action (ignoring the Coin cost for placing a Passenger), advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.

entries.term: Hospital de La Santa Creu i de Sant Pau
entries.definition: Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to perform a Build an Intersection action (ignoring any Coin cost including the printed board cost), advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to perform a Build Wide Street/Narrow Streets action, advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to perform a Place Cobblestone action (ignoring the adjacency rule, allowing placement anywhere on the Sidewalk), advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to advance twice on your Sagrada Familia track (gaining all corresponding benefits), advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Public Service: pay 3/2/1 Coins to perform a Take a Modernisme Project action (ignoring the Cloth cost of placing a Modernisme tile), advance 2 on Cerda track, and gain 10/6/3 VP.
Given to the starting player. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player each round.
Contains all Citizen tokens not pre-placed on tracks. Players draw 2 Citizens from the bag at the end of each turn. Citizens are drawn randomly and kept hidden from other players.
A reference card (4 included) summarizing the turn structure, action icons, and scoring rules. Keep one near your player board during the game.

The measure of success in Barcelona. Earned throughout the game from actions, buildings, street connections, Cerda Scorings, and at end of game from Cobblestones, Passengers, and Modernisme tiles. Tracked on the scoring track around the main board.
At end of game, score VP equal to the rightmost visible VP number on your Warehouse (revealed by removing Cobblestones during the game). More Cobblestones removed = higher VP.
At end of game, score VP equal to the number printed on the rightmost uncovered Passenger space on your player board. More Passengers placed on the board = higher VP.

At end of game, evaluate each Modernisme tile: count how many times the condition is met, multiply by the VP value, then multiply by the corresponding Modernisme marker position. Sum all tiles for total Modernisme VP.

If VP is tied at end of game, break ties in order: furthest on the Cerda track, then furthest on the Sagrada Familia track, then most Building markers on the main board. If still tied, players share the victory.
